dialogue.speaker: joseph
dialogue.englishText: Up, follow the men, and when you overtake them, say to them, 'Why have you repaid evil for good?'

dialogue.speaker: joseph
dialogue.englishText: Is it not from this that my lord drinks, and by this that he practices divination? You have done evil in doing this

dialogue.speaker: jacobs-sons
dialogue.englishText: Why does my lord speak such words as these? Far be it from your servants to do such a thing!

dialogue.speaker: jacobs-sons
dialogue.englishText: Behold, the money that we found in the mouth of our sacks we brought back to you from the land of Canaan. How then could we steal silver or gold from your lord's house?

dialogue.speaker: jacobs-sons
dialogue.englishText: Whichever of your servants is found with it shall die, and we also will be my lord's servants

dialogue.speaker: josephs-steward
dialogue.englishText: Let it be as you say: he with whom it is found shall be my servant, and the rest of you shall be innocent

dialogue.speaker: joseph
dialogue.englishText: What deed is this that you have done? Do you not know that a man like me can indeed practice divination?

dialogue.speaker: judah
dialogue.englishText: What shall we say to my lord? What shall we speak? Or how can we clear ourselves? God has found out the guilt of your servants; behold, we are my lord's servants, both we and he also in whose hand the cup has been found

dialogue.speaker: joseph
dialogue.englishText: Far be it from me that I should do so! Only the man in whose hand the cup was found shall be my servant. But as for you, go up in peace to your father

dialogue.speaker: judah
dialogue.englishText: Oh, my lord, please let your servant speak a word in my lord's ears, and let not your anger burn against your servant, for you are like Pharaoh himself

dialogue.speaker: judah
dialogue.englishText: My lord asked his servants, saying, 'Have you a father or a brother?'

dialogue.speaker: judah
dialogue.englishText: And we said to my lord, 'We have a father, an old man, and a young child of his old age. His brother is dead, and he alone is left of his mother's children, and his father loves him'

dialogue.speaker: judah
dialogue.englishText: Then you said to your servants, 'Bring him down to me, that I may set my eyes on him'

dialogue.speaker: judah
dialogue.englishText: We said to my lord, 'The boy cannot leave his father, for if he should leave his father, his father would die'

dialogue.speaker: judah
dialogue.englishText: Then you said to your servants, 'Unless your youngest brother comes down with you, you shall not see my face again'

dialogue.speaker: judah
dialogue.englishText: When we went back to your servant my father, we told him the words of my lord

dialogue.speaker: judah
dialogue.englishText: And our father said, 'Go again, buy us a little food'

dialogue.speaker: judah
dialogue.englishText: We said, 'We cannot go down. If our youngest brother goes with us, then we will go down. For we cannot see the man's face unless our youngest brother is with us'

dialogue.speaker: judah
dialogue.englishText: Then your servant my father said to us, 'You know that my wife bore me two sons'

dialogue.speaker: judah
dialogue.englishText: One left me, and I said, Surely he has been torn to pieces, and I have never seen him since

dialogue.speaker: judah
dialogue.englishText: If you take this one also from me, and harm happens to him, you will bring down my gray hairs in evil to Sheol

dialogue.speaker: judah
dialogue.englishText: Now therefore, as soon as I come to your servant my father, and the boy is not with us, since his life is bound up in the boy's life

dialogue.speaker: judah
dialogue.englishText: As soon as he sees that the boy is not with us, he will die, and your servants will bring down the gray hairs of your servant our father with sorrow to Sheol

dialogue.speaker: judah
dialogue.englishText: For your servant became a pledge of safety for the boy to my father, saying, 'If I do not bring him back to you, then I shall bear the blame before my father all my life'

dialogue.speaker: judah
dialogue.englishText: Now therefore, please let your servant remain instead of the boy as a servant to my lord, and let the boy go back with his brothers

dialogue.speaker: judah
dialogue.englishText: For how can I go back to my father if the boy is not with me? I fear to see the evil that would find my father
